prerequisites: A Notion account with Workspace Owner status in the workspace whose content you want to read.
A Notion connection (integration) created in the Notion Developer portal, which provides either an integration token or OAuth client credentials.
The Notion pages and databases you want to sync explicitly shared with the connection (open each page, use the More menu, then Add connections, and select your connection).
A start_date value to bound which records are synced (the connector only replicates data created or updated on or after this date).

authMethods.name: Access Token (internal integration / personal access token)
authMethods.summary: Maps to the credentials.token secret field. Create an internal connection in the Notion Developer portal (Workspace Owner required), then copy the installation access token from the Configuration tab. Notion sends this token as a Bearer token in the Authorization header. Share each target page or database with the connection so the API can access it.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (public integration)
authMethods.summary: Maps to the credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, and credentials.access_token secret fields. Create a public connection in the Notion Developer portal with redirect URIs, then run the OAuth authorization code flow: direct the user to the authorization URL, exchange the returned code at https://api.notion.com/v1/oauth/token using HTTP Basic auth with the client_id and client_secret, and store the resulting access_token for API requests.

setupSteps.title: Create a Notion connection (integration)
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Notion as a Workspace Owner and open the Notion Developer portal. For token-based auth, create an Internal connection, name it, select the workspace it can be installed in, and copy the installation access token from the Configuration tab. For OAuth, create a Public connection, configure redirect URIs and the installation scope, and note the generated client_id and client_secret.

setupSteps.title: Share pages and databases with the connection
setupSteps.body: Open each Notion page or database you want to sync, click the More menu (three dots) in the top right, choose Add connections, and select your connection. The connector can only read content that has been explicitly shared this way. If API requests fail, confirm the connection has access to the target pages.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials to the connector
setupSteps.body: In the connector configuration, fill the credentials field. For token auth, supply credentials.token with the integration / access token. For OAuth, supply credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, and credentials.access_token obtained from the authorization code flow.

setupSteps.title: Set sync configuration
setupSteps.body: Set start_date to the earliest creation/update timestamp you want to replicate (records older than this are skipped). Optionally adjust num_workers to control the number of concurrent workers used during extraction.
